seoTitle: How to Automatically Upload Files to Google Drive With n8n
metaDescription: Learn how to connect Google Drive to n8n using OAuth2 credentials and automate uploading files into specific cloud folders.
intro: Automating file uploads to Google Drive with n8n means capturing files from an earlier step in your workflow and routing them through the Google Drive node once your credentials are connected. This guide covers registering your app in Google Cloud, linking credentials, and configuring the upload operation.

steps.heading: Bring a file into your workflow
steps.body: Capture the file you want to upload from an earlier node as binary data.

steps.heading: Add the Google Drive node
steps.body: Place the node onto your canvas where the upload should happen.

steps.heading: Set up your OAuth credentials
steps.body: Create a credential profile in n8n and register an app in the Google Cloud Console to generate your client keys.

steps.heading: Complete the authentication
steps.body: Paste your client ID and secret back into n8n and finish the authorization handshake.

steps.heading: Configure the upload and test
steps.body: Set the operation to upload a file, specify the target folder, and run a test to confirm it lands correctly.

faq.question: Do I need a Google Cloud account to connect Drive to n8n?
faq.answer: Yes, you'll need to register an app in the Google Cloud Console to generate the OAuth credentials n8n requires.

faq.question: Can I control which folder files upload into?
faq.answer: Yes, the Google Drive node lets you specify a target folder ID for uploads.

faq.question: Can I name files dynamically based on workflow data?
faq.answer: Yes, you can map dynamic values into the file name when configuring the upload operation.

faq.question: What kind of files can I upload with this integration?
faq.answer: Any binary file data captured earlier in your workflow can be routed into the Google Drive upload operation.
